title: aconitum vulparia subsp. neapolitanum
intro: Aconitum vulparia subsp. neapolitanum is a subspecies of the Aconitum vulparia plant, commonly known as wolf's bane or monk's hood, which belongs to the Aconitum genus of flowering plants in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to numb pain and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Aconitum vulparia subsp. neapolitanum include its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis, fever, and skin irritations, as well as its use as a sedative and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, have been traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic nature.
